name: 1St Bergen
description_short: 1st Bergen Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving Bergen County, NJ since 2009 with checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
description_long:
1st Bergen Federal Credit Union received its federal charter from the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) in 2009, making it the first credit union in New Jersey to receive a federal charter in more than 21 years. The institution was certified as a Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) in 2011 and is designated as a "low-income" credit union by the NCUA. Since its founding, 1st Bergen has grown from 60 members and $90,000 in assets to over 2,500 members and $10 million in assets while maintaining its commitment to personalized service.

1st Bergen offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal and business checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), youth accounts, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and credit-building products. Members can access online and mobile banking platforms, Zelle transfer services, loan calculators, and merchant services/point-of-sale solutions for business members. The credit union also provides financial wellness resources and a Future Dream Program.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, 1st Bergen operates as a member-owned cooperative where members elect the board of directors and share in the institution's success. The organization explicitly targets individuals and organizations in Bergen County, NJ, as well as those served by partner organizations like Greater Bergen Community Action and OCEAN Inc. Their CDFI designation and low-income credit union status reflect a mission focused on underserved communities.

Membership requires only a $5 initial deposit, two forms of identification, and proof of address. However, membership is geographically restricted to Bergen County, NJ residents or those with specific affiliations. While 1st Bergen offers standard credit union services, the website provides limited detail on specific rates, loan terms, or competitive advantages beyond their community development focus. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: CDFI-certified and NCUA low-income designated credit union with explicit community development mission
Very low membership barrier at $5 initial deposit with straightforward application process
Member-owned cooperative structure means members elect leadership and share in profits
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and business services
Mobile app and online banking available for account access and management
First credit union in New Jersey to receive federal charter in 21+ years (since 2009)
cons: Membership geographically restricted to Bergen County, NJ or specific partner organization affiliations only
Website provides no details on APRs, interest rates, or loan terms for comparison
Limited hours (M-F 8:00am-3:30pm) with no mention of weekend or extended availability
No information on ATM network access or surcharge policies
Small institution ($10 million in assets) may have fewer resources than larger banks
best_for: Bergen County, NJ residents seeking community-focused banking with a low-income designation
Small business owners needing personalized lending and merchant services in New Jersey
Members of partnered organizations (Greater Bergen Community Action, OCEAN Inc.) seeking affordable financial services
Individuals looking to build or rebuild credit within a credit union structure
diagnosis: 1st Bergen Federal Credit Union is best for residents of Bergen County, NJ seeking community-focused banking from a not-for-profit institution with CDFI certification and a low-income credit union mission. The main caveat is strict geographic membership restrictions and lack of publicly available rate information, requiring direct contact for pricing comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
